Earnings yield 2.74% still below risk-free rate, limiting upside from valuation alone

TTM EPS through Q3 2025 of ~$19.49 grew only ~7% YoY ($4.37+$5.07+$5.24+$5.07 vs prior TTM ~$18.22) — solid but modest growth that doesn't justify the premium multiple
Stock at $322.22 (+34% over past year) with earnings yield of only ~6.0% vs 10.3% at the Dec 2023 entry point — slim compensation for downside risk

Prior bearish views since mid-2024 were directionally correct on valuation exhaustion; the tariff-driven April 2025 dip to $210 (P/E ~11x) was a missed entry that has since fully recovered to stretche

trace.final_explanation: openclaw_per_ticker: 30/30 tickers produced a view via openclaw.

AAPL.final_explanation: AAPL thesis revised upward: prior bearish call on PE expansion without fundamentals was wrong — earnings growth accelerated and PE compressed, shifting risk/reward modestly positive but not compelling at 36x trailing.

AMZN.final_explanation: AMZN remains compelling: P/E has compressed from ~47x to ~33x over the past year while TTM earnings grew ~29% and revenue accelerated to +19% YoY, yet the stock is flat — the market has not re-rated the improving fundamentals. AWS re-acceleration, margin expansion, and approaching Q4 earnings create a favorable risk/reward setup.

BA.final_explanation: Boeing's turnaround narrative is strong but the income statement hasn't confirmed it — stock has re-rated while profitability regressed, leaving risk-reward mildly unfavorable.

CAT.final_explanation: CAT has a genuine AI data center catalyst but at 29.4x TTM P/E with compressing margins and $1.5-1.8B tariff headwinds, the risk-reward is unfavorable for a cyclical industrial priced for perfection.

CRM.final_explanation: CRM offers moderate risk-adjusted reward on improving fundamentals (margin expansion, AI monetization, revenue reacceleration) but the 16% bounce from Oct lows reduces asymmetry at 41.7x P/E; Informatica integration adds execution risk to an otherwise constructive setup.

IBM.final_explanation: IBM's valuation has further stretched from my prior bearish call, with P/E approaching 50x on mid-single-digit growth — the risk-reward remains firmly negative despite solid operational execution.

JNJ.final_explanation: JNJ quality compounder thesis intact — strong execution, expanding margins, solid pipeline — but 43% YTD rally and P/E re-rating to 20x have consumed the valuation discount, leaving ~10% upside at fair value and shifting risk/reward to balanced.

JPM.final_explanation: JPM remains a best-in-class franchise but the valuation re-rating from 9.7x to 16.6x P/E over two years has exhausted the margin of safety; at the top of its 15-year historical range with only modest earnings growth, risk-reward is mildly negative.

MCD.final_explanation: MCD is a quality compounder with genuine earnings reacceleration that the market hasn't fully rewarded, but fair valuation at 26x trailing P/E limits risk-adjusted upside to modest levels.

MMM.final_explanation: MMM has completed its post-Solventum rerating to ~20x P/E; with 3-4% organic growth, a stressed balance sheet (D/E 2.7x, $13.5B litigation reserves), and no clear catalyst, risk-adjusted reward is mildly negative from here.

MSFT.final_explanation: MSFT risk/reward has shifted mildly positive after valuation correction from 38x to 34x P/E combined with accelerating growth, but premium valuation still demands sustained execution.

PG.final_explanation: PG presents the most attractive valuation entry point in 2+ years: PE compressed from 28.7x (Jan 2025) to ~22.5x while fundamentals have meaningfully improved, with Q1 FY2026 revenue +13.2% YoY, operating margin expanding to 26.2%, and ROE surging to 36.6%. The stock is down 20% from ATH despite the strongest fundamental quarter in the dataset — a rare divergence that creates favorable risk/reward.
